note: Fixture for the frontend replay. Inner `finding` and `verdict` objects match the engine's pydantic shapes EXACTLY (premortem/schemas.py: Finding, Verdict, Citation). The outer envelope (timeline, greenlight, memory_appended) is a forward-compatible wrapper the replay needs but the engine's Result does not yet carry: Result holds only FINAL verdicts, the greenlight pass is not yet implemented, and runs are not yet persisted to JSON. When the engine emits a real run (Task C), it maps into this envelope.

protocol.name: Phase 2 anti-tau monoclonal antibody in PSP
protocol.population: Probable PSP-Richardson syndrome per MDS-PSP 2017 criteria
protocol.eligibility: Age 40-80; PSPRS within a defined window; able to walk 5 steps unaided; CSF or tau-PET confirmation of tauopathy

intervention.mechanism: binds extracellular N-terminal tau to block trans-synaptic seeding and spread

protocol.primary_endpoint: Change from baseline in PSP Rating Scale (PSPRS) at 52 weeks

powering.assumed_effect: 30% slowing of PSPRS progression vs placebo

findings.title: Extracellular N-terminal tau engagement does not translate to clinical benefit in PSP — this exact mechanism already failed.
findings.verdict: The intervention binds extracellular N-terminal tau to block trans-synaptic spread — the same mechanism as gosuranemab. In PASSPORT, gosuranemab achieved near-complete target engagement yet produced no clinical benefit on PSPRS. Target engagement here is not evidence of efficacy; the one trial that engaged this target proved engagement and benefit are decoupled in PSP.
findings.quantitative_threshold: PASSPORT: ~98% reduction in free CSF N-terminal tau (target engaged) yet adjusted PSPRS difference vs placebo of ~0.2 points at week 52, p=0.85 (not significant). This protocol is powered for 30% PSPRS slowing on the identical mechanism.

citation.title: PASSPORT: A Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Gosuranemab (BIIB092) in Participants With Progressive Supranuclear Palsy

findings.remediation: Do not run as designed. A defensible program must target a differentiated tau species — seed-competent MTBR (microtubule-binding-region) tau, or total-tau lowering via ASO — rather than re-binding the extracellular N-terminal epitope PASSPORT already cleared without benefit. Absent a differentiated translational rationale, this is a do-not-run.

findings.title: The N-terminal anti-tau antibody class is a graveyard — a second independent failure corroborates the kill.
findings.verdict: Gosuranemab is not an isolated miss. Tilavonemab (AbbVie ABBV-8E12), an independent N-terminal-directed anti-tau monoclonal, was also tested in PSP and stopped for futility with no benefit on PSPRS. Two independent antibodies against the same extracellular N-terminal target both failed — this is a class signal, not a molecule-specific one.
findings.quantitative_threshold: Tilavonemab Phase 2 in PSP (N≈378) terminated at the pre-specified interim for futility; no significant PSPRS benefit vs placebo. Combined with PASSPORT, that is 2/2 N-terminal antibodies failing in PSP.

citation.title: A Study of ABBV-8E12 (Tilavonemab) in Participants With Progressive Supranuclear Palsy (PSP)

findings.remediation: A same-epitope antibody cannot claim differentiation from the failed class. Require a distinct molecular target (intracellular/seed-competent tau, total-tau lowering) before committing IND resources.

findings.title: Powered for a 30% effect no anti-tau agent has ever shown against a noisy scale at N=200.
findings.verdict: The 30% PSPRS-slowing assumption is not anchored to any positive precedent — every anti-tau agent tested in PSP has shown ~0% slowing. PSPRS progresses ~10-11 points/year with high inter-patient variance; at N=200 the trial is underpowered to detect the more realistic <=20% effect, and is sized to an effect that has never been observed.
findings.quantitative_threshold: Assumed 30% slowing, N=200, 1:1, alpha 0.05, 80% power. Natural-history PSPRS progression ~10-11 pts/yr (SD large). A realistic 20% effect roughly doubles the required N; the current design has no interim futility gate.

citation.title: Davunetide (AL-108-231) in Predicted Tauopathies — PSP cohort; placebo-arm PSPRS progression and variance

findings.remediation: Add a pre-specified interim futility gate; re-power for a realistic <=20% slowing (larger N) or adopt a Bayesian adaptive design; do not power to an effect size with zero positive precedent in the indication.

findings.title: Mandatory tau-PET/CSF confirmation plus ambulation cut an already-tiny pool below feasible enrollment.
findings.verdict: PSP prevalence is ~5-7/100,000. Requiring tau-PET OR CSF confirmation of tauopathy AND the ability to walk 5 steps unaided stacks two restrictive gates onto a rare population, inflating screen-fail and slowing accrual. Comparable PSP trials enrolled slowly even with broader criteria.
findings.quantitative_threshold: Prevalence ~5-7/100,000. PASSPORT enrolled N=486 across ~60 sites in multiple countries over ~2 years with clinical (not mandatory-PET) criteria; layering mandatory confirmatory imaging/CSF onto that materially raises screen-fail rate and timeline.

citation.title: PASSPORT enrollment record (N, sites, duration) and PSP epidemiology — ClinicalTrials.gov

findings.remediation: Enroll on MDS-PSP 2017 'probable PSP-Richardson syndrome' clinical criteria; make tau-PET/CSF confirmatory (not gating) or a sub-study; broaden the ambulation requirement and expand to >50 sites to hit timeline.

findings.title: CSF tau / NfL / tau-PET are engagement markers, not approvable surrogates — the secondary stack cannot carry the efficacy argument.
findings.verdict: The secondary endpoints (CSF total tau, plasma NfL, tau-PET SUVR) are positioned as if they support an efficacy claim. None is an FDA-validated surrogate for clinical benefit in PSP; there is no approved DMT and no qualified surrogate. A biomarker move without PSPRS benefit is not an approvable result.
findings.quantitative_threshold: Zero biomarkers qualified as surrogate endpoints for PSP efficacy (FDA). PASSPORT moved engagement biomarkers (~98% free-tau reduction) with 0 clinical benefit — direct proof biomarker movement != benefit here.

citation.title: FDA: no approved disease-modifying therapy and no qualified surrogate endpoint for PSP (neurodegeneration endpoint guidance)

findings.remediation: Demote biomarkers to go/no-go target-engagement readouts feeding an interim decision; keep PSPRS as the sole primary efficacy endpoint; do not file an efficacy narrative resting on biomarker movement.

findings.title: Davunetide is a third independent anti-tau failure in PSP — corroborating, but the first draft overstated it (negative control).
findings.verdict: Davunetide (a tau-directed neuroprotective agent) was tested in PSP and showed no benefit on PSPRS or function — a third independent anti-tau approach that engaged plausibly yet failed. It corroborates the class risk that motivates the kill.
findings.quantitative_threshold: Davunetide PSP trial (N=313, 52 weeks): no significant difference vs placebo on PSPRS or SEADL (co-primaries). Adds a third independent null to the anti-tau PSP record.

citation.title: Davunetide (AL-108-231) Phase 2/3 in PSP — Boxer et al., Lancet Neurology 2014

findings.remediation: Treat the anti-tau PSP record as 3/3 independent failures (gosuranemab, tilavonemab, davunetide) when judging whether an N-terminal antibody is differentiated; require a distinct mechanism.

timeline.label: Builder drafts 6 findings -> verifier grades each in a fresh, independent context (rubric.md + one finding, no builder reasoning).

g1.reason: NCT03068468 (PASSPORT, gosuranemab in PSP) retrieved; real, specific, public.

g2.reason: Verdict matches source: near-complete target engagement, no PSPRS benefit at wk52.

g3.reason: Carries thresholds: ~98% free-tau reduction, ~0.2-pt PSPRS diff, p=0.85, vs 30% powering.

g4.reason: Specific fix (differentiate to MTBR/total-tau) or reasoned do-not-run with conditions.

g1.reason: NCT02985879 (tilavonemab/ABBV-8E12 in PSP) retrieved; real and specific.

g2.reason: Source confirms futility termination, no PSPRS benefit; 2/2 N-terminal antibodies failed.

g3.reason: Quantified: N~378, interim futility stop; combined 2/2 class failures.

g4.reason: Actionable: require distinct molecular target before IND commitment.

g1.reason: NCT01110720 placebo-arm PSPRS progression/variance retrieved as the powering anchor.

g2.reason: Verdict follows: 30% effect has no positive precedent; N=200 underpowered for <=20%.

g3.reason: Thresholds present: ~10-11 pts/yr progression, 30% vs realistic <=20%, N implications.

g4.reason: Concrete: add futility interim, re-power or Bayesian adaptive.

g1.reason: PASSPORT enrollment record + PSP prevalence retrievable; sources specific.

g2.reason: Verdict matches: stacked confirmatory gates raise screen-fail on a rare population.

g4.reason: Specific: switch to MDS-PSP clinical criteria, confirmatory-not-gating PET, expand sites.

g1.reason: FDA neurodegeneration endpoint posture (no qualified PSP surrogate) retrievable.

g2.reason: Verdict matches: biomarkers are engagement, not approvable surrogates in PSP.

g3.reason: Quantified: 0 qualified surrogates; PASSPORT ~98% engagement, 0 benefit.

g4.reason: Actionable: demote biomarkers to go/no-go, keep PSPRS sole primary.

g1.reason: NCT01110720 (davunetide in PSP, Boxer 2014) retrieved; real and specific.

g2.reason: OVERSTATES SOURCE: draft claimed davunetide 'worsened PSPRS (harm signal).' The trial showed NO significant difference vs placebo — null, not harm. Verdict does not follow from the source.

g4.reason: Remediation built on the false 'harm' premise ('anti-aggregation is dangerous') is not supported and not actionable.

timeline.label: Builder revises ONLY A3-3 (corrects the overstated claim to what the source actually says) -> verifier re-grades all findings.

g1.reason: NCT01110720 powering anchor retrieved; unchanged PASS.

g2.reason: No positive precedent for 30%; unchanged PASS.

g1.reason: Enrollment + prevalence sources retrieved; unchanged PASS.

g2.reason: Stacked gates raise screen-fail; unchanged PASS.

g4.reason: MDS-PSP criteria + site expansion; unchanged PASS.

g1.reason: FDA surrogate posture retrieved; unchanged PASS.

g4.reason: Demote biomarkers to go/no-go; unchanged PASS.

g2.reason: FIXED: revised verdict now states davunetide showed NO benefit (null) — matches Boxer 2014 exactly. Overstatement removed.

g3.reason: Quantified: N=313, 52 wk, co-primaries null; adds a third independent null.

g4.reason: Actionable: treat anti-tau PSP record as 3/3 failures when judging differentiation.

memory_appended.pattern: Extracellular N-terminal tau engagement -> no PSP clinical benefit (PASSPORT). Generalizes to: target-engagement-without-downstream-effect — engagement is necessary, not sufficient.
Same-epitope 'me-too' biologic inherits the class failure (tilavonemab corroborates gosuranemab). Generalizes to: a molecule sharing a failed target's mechanism cannot claim differentiation.
Powering to an effect size with zero positive precedent in-indication. Generalizes to: anchor power calculations to observed effects, not aspirational ones; gate with interim futility.

greenlight.decision: DO NOT RUN AS DESIGNED — but a defensible configuration exists.
greenlight.summary: The protocol as written (extracellular N-terminal anti-tau antibody, powered for 30% PSPRS slowing at N=200) is a do-not-run: it re-runs a mechanism proven inert in PSP across 3 independent trials, on optimistic powering, on an over-narrow population. A greenlight path exists only if it clears the SAME four gates as the kill — every lever below was verifier-passed. The path to yes requires (1) a differentiated tau species, (2) phenotype enrichment, (3) realistic, futility-gated powering.

levers.title: Repurpose salsalate — differentiated mechanism, collapsed IND burden.
levers.verdict: Salsalate is an approved anti-inflammatory (salicylate prodrug) that inhibits p300 acetyltransferase, reducing pathogenic tau acetylation — a mechanism distinct from the dead N-terminal-antibody class. As an approved drug it brings known safety/PK/tox, collapsing IND burden. Prior human/preclinical tau-acetylation signal exists, though PSP-specific efficacy is unproven.
levers.quantitative_threshold: Approved drug (existing safety DB); mechanistic link to 4R-tau via p300/tau-acetylation; small open-label PSP/FTD exposure exists. Lever is a futility-gated signal-finding bet, not a proven therapy.

citation.title: Salsalate in Progressive Supranuclear Palsy — open-label pilot (tau acetylation rationale)

levers.remediation: Run salsalate behind a biomarker-gated go/no-go interim; advance only on a pre-specified target-engagement signal.

g1.reason: Approval status and PSP pilot retrievable.

g2.reason: Claims bounded to 'candidate/unproven' — no overstatement of efficacy.

g3.reason: Three grounded facts: approval, mechanism, prior signal.

levers.title: Lithium as a GSK-3β-rationale CANDIDATE — held to the same bar, not adopted as proven.
levers.verdict: Lithium is approved and inhibits GSK-3β, reducing tau phosphorylation — a plausible 4R-tau mechanistic link. It is a repurposing CANDIDATE only: there is no positive PSP efficacy trial, so the optimism must be hedged exactly as a risk would be. This lever passes only because it is framed as a futility-gated bet, not a claim of benefit.
levers.quantitative_threshold: Approved drug; GSK-3β -> tau-phosphorylation mechanistic link; prior tauopathy human exposure with NO demonstrated PSP benefit to date. Admitted as candidate-for-testing, not proven therapy.

citation.title: Lithium in Progressive Supranuclear Palsy and Corticobasal Degeneration — tolerability/feasibility

levers.remediation: Only as a dedicated futility-gated Phase 2 signal-finding arm with a pre-specified stopping rule; do not position as established therapy.

g2.reason: Honestly framed as candidate with NO proven benefit — earlier 'proven to slow PSP' draft was FAILED and corrected (optimism clears the same bar).

g3.reason: Approval, GSK-3β mechanism, prior-exposure facts all grounded.

g4.reason: Specific: futility-gated arm with stopping rule.

levers.title: Redesigned IND: differentiated target + phenotype enrichment + Bayesian adaptive + futility gate.
levers.verdict: Independent of the molecule, the trial design itself can be made defensible: switch the primary target to seed-competent MTBR / total-tau lowering (escaping the N-terminal graveyard), enrich for probable PSP-Richardson syndrome, adopt a Bayesian adaptive design powered for a realistic <=20% slowing, and install a pre-specified biomarker-gated interim futility gate so the trial can stop early for the right reasons.
levers.quantitative_threshold: Re-power for <=20% PSPRS slowing (vs the unprecedented 30%); add interim futility at ~50% information; biomarkers (CSF tau, NfL, tau-PET) as go/no-go engagement readouts, PSPRS as sole primary.

citation.title: PASSPORT (engagement-without-benefit) as the design constraint motivating futility-gated, differentiated-target trials

levers.remediation: Adopt as the greenlight design: differentiated tau species, RS enrichment, Bayesian adaptive, interim futility, biomarkers as go/no-go only.

g1.reason: Grounded in the PASSPORT engagement-vs-benefit record.

g2.reason: Design claims follow from the cited failure mode.

g3.reason: Quantified: <=20% effect, interim at ~50% info, named endpoints.

g4.reason: Itself a concrete, parameter-level design specification.

config.mechanism: SWITCH: extracellular N-terminal antibody -> seed-competent MTBR / total-tau-lowering ASO, OR repurpose salsalate / lithium (differentiated, IND burden collapsed).
config.population: Enrich for probable PSP-Richardson syndrome (MDS-PSP 2017); tau-PET/CSF confirmatory not gating; broaden ambulation; expand to >50 sites.
config.endpoint_and_powering: PSPRS sole primary; re-power for realistic <=20% slowing via Bayesian adaptive design; pre-specified biomarker-gated interim futility gate; biomarkers (CSF tau, NfL, tau-PET) as go/no-go engagement only.
config.bottom_line: Do NOT run the antibody protocol as designed. A trial becomes defensible only when it (1) targets a differentiated tau species, (2) enriches phenotype, and (3) powers realistically behind a futility gate — every one of those levers cleared the same verifier the kill-list did.
